While Ephraim maintained a reverent fear of God and worshiped Him in that fear, he was very significant for as long as he did so. When Ephraim abandoned God and followed idolatry, he declined. Let those who sacrifice idols kiss them, as a sign of their adoration, affection, and obedience to them; but the Lord will not give his glory to another, and therefore all who worship images will be ashamed. No lasting comfort is to be expected anywhere except in God. God not only cared for the Israelites in the wilderness, he gave them possession of Canaan, a good land; but worldly prosperity, when it fuels people's pride, makes them forgetful of God. Therefore the Lord would meet them in just punishment, like the most terrible beast that lived in their forests. Misused kindness calls for greater severity.

Israel destroyed himself through his rebellion; but he could not save himself, because his help came only from the Lord. This can be applied to the case of spiritual redemption, from the lost state into which all have fallen by willful sins. God often gives what we desire sinfully, even when displeased. It is the happiness of believers that, whether God gives or takes away, it is all done in love. But it is the misery of the wicked that, whether God gives or takes away, it is all done in wrath, and nothing brings comfort. Unless sinners repent and believe the gospel, anguish will soon come upon them. The prophecy about Israel’s ruin as a nation also showed there would be a merciful and powerful intervention by God to save a remnant of them. Yet this was only a shadow of the ransom of true Israel, through the death, burial, and resurrection of Christ. He will destroy death and the grave. The Lord will not regret his purpose or promise. However, in the meantime, Israel would be devastated because of her sins. Without fruitfulness in good works, springing from the Holy Spirit, all other fruitfulness will be as empty as the uncertain riches of the world. God’s wrath will wither its branches; its sprigs will dry up; it will come to nothing. Woes more terrible than any from the most cruel warfare will fall on those who rebel against God. From such miseries and from sin, the cause of them, may the Lord deliver us.
